attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Minima
2	
3	**Recommend a cheaper LLM model for each task, so LLM-driven workflows spend fewer tokens
4	without losing the quality the task actually needs.**
5	
6	Minima **only recommends** — it never proxies a call, runs a model, rewrites a prompt, or
7	caches. It is a stack-agnostic advice layer backed by [Mubit](https://docs.mubit.ai) memory:
8	ask which model to use, run that model yourself, then tell Minima how it went. Because it
9	sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, **it adds zero latency to your real LLM
10	request.**
11	
12	```
13	  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
14	   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce memory)
15	        ▲                                                           │
16	        └──────────────  recommendations get sharper  ─────────────┘
17	```
18	
19	## Why it works
20	
21	Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) memory. Every `POST /v1/feedback` writes a `task → model → outcome` record; every `POST /v1/recommend` recalls the most similar past records and picks the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar. The longer it runs, the sharper the picks.
22	
23	A `cost_quality_tradeoff` slider (0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality) moves the
24	bar. When memory is thin or conflicting, Minima can escalate to a cheap-LLM reasoner
25	(configurable, off by default).
26	
27	### Cost ranking that reflects reality
28	
29	A flat token estimate assumes a fixed completion length, so it ignores reasoning/thinking
30	tokens and mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima
31	ranks candidates by what they **really** cost, choosing one basis for the whole candidate
32	set:
33	
34	- **rescaled** (best) — this request's input priced + the model's *observed* output-token
35	  behavior; size-exact **and** reasoning-aware.
36	- **observed** — robust median of realized `$/call` from recalled outcomes.
37	- **estimate** (cold start) — token estimate from catalog prices.
38	
39	The basis climbs `estimate → observed → rescaled` as your `/feedback` calls accumulate
40	realized tokens and cost. See [Concepts → Cost-basis tiers](docs/concepts.md#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled).
41	
42	## Endpoints
43	
44	| Endpoint | Purpose |
45	|----------|---------|
46	| `POST /v1/recommend` | Recommend a model for one task. |
47	| `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` | Recommend a model per step of a multi-step workflow. |
48	| `POST /v1/feedback` | Report an outcome and close the learning loop. |
49	| `GET  /v1/models` | The current model catalog (cost + capability priors). |
50	| `GET  /v1/strategies` | Rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace (explainability). |
51	| `GET  /v1/health` | Service, Mubit, catalog, and reasoner status. |
52	| `POST\|GET\|DELETE /v1/admin/tenants` | Tenant provisioning (multi-tenant mode only). |
53	
54	Full schemas, fields, warnings, and error formats: **[API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)**.
55	
56	## Quickstart
57	
58	```bash
59	uv sync --extra dev
60	cp .env.example .env                       # set MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MUBIT_ENDPOINT if not local)

file.content:
# Minima

**Recommend a cheaper LLM model for each task, so LLM-driven workflows spend fewer tokens
without losing the quality the task actually needs.**

Minima **only recommends** — it never proxies a call, runs a model, rewrites a prompt, or
caches. It is a stack-agnostic advice layer backed by [Mubit](https://docs.mubit.ai) memory:
ask which model to use, run that model yourself, then tell Minima how it went. Because it
sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, **it adds zero latency to your real LLM
request.**

```
  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce memory)
        ▲                                                           │
        └──────────────  recommendations get sharper  ─────────────┘
```

## Why it works

Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) memory. Every `POST /v1/feedback` writes a `task → model → outcome` record; every `POST /v1/recommend` recalls the most similar past records and picks the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar. The longer it runs, the sharper the picks.

A `cost_quality_tradeoff` slider (0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality) moves the
bar. When memory is thin or conflicting, Minima can escalate to a cheap-LLM reasoner
(configurable, off by default).

### Cost ranking that reflects reality

A flat token estimate assumes a fixed completion length, so it ignores reasoning/thinking
tokens and mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima
ranks candidates by what they **really** cost, choosing one basis for the whole candidate
set:

- **rescaled** (best) — this request's input priced + the model's *observed* output-token
  behavior; size-exact **and** reasoning-aware.
- **observed** — robust median of realized `$/call` from recalled outcomes.
- **estimate** (cold start) — token estimate from catalog prices.

The basis climbs `estimate → observed → rescaled` as your `/feedback` calls accumulate
realized tokens and cost. See [Concepts → Cost-basis tiers](docs/concepts.md#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled).

## Endpoints

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `POST /v1/recommend` | Recommend a model for one task. |
| `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` | Recommend a model per step of a multi-step workflow. |
| `POST /v1/feedback` | Report an outcome and close the learning loop. |
| `GET  /v1/models` | The current model catalog (cost + capability priors). |
| `GET  /v1/strategies` | Rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace (explainability). |
| `GET  /v1/health` | Service, Mubit, catalog, and reasoner status. |
| `POST\|GET\|DELETE /v1/admin/tenants` | Tenant provisioning (multi-tenant mode only). |

Full schemas, fields, warnings, and error formats: **[API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)**.

## Quickstart

```bash
uv sync --extra dev
cp .env.example .env                       # set MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MUBIT_ENDPOINT if not local)

content.content:
1	"""Recommendation endpoints (per-call and per-workflow-step)."""
2

"""Recommendation endpoints (per-call and per-workflow-step)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from minima.api.auth import get_tenant
from minima.schemas.recommend import RecommendRequest, RecommendResponse
from minima.schemas.workflow import (
    StepRecommendation,
    WorkflowRequest,
    WorkflowResponse,
)
from minima.tenancy.context import TenantContext

router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1", tags=["recommend"])


@router.post("/recommend", response_model=RecommendResponse)
async def recommend(
    req: RecommendRequest, tenant: TenantContext = Depends(get_tenant)
) -> RecommendResponse:
    return await tenant.recommender.recommend(req)


@router.post("/recommend/workflow", response_model=WorkflowResponse)
async def recommend_workflow(
    req: WorkflowRequest, tenant: TenantContext = Depends(get_tenant)
) -> WorkflowResponse:
    rec = tenant.recommender
    steps: list[StepRecommendation] = []
    total = 0.0
    premium = 0.0
    confidences: list[float] = []

    for step in req.steps:
        constraints = (
            step.constraints.merged_over(req.constraints) if step.constraints else req.constraints
        )
        sub = RecommendRequest(
            task=step.task,
            cost_quality_tradeoff=req.cost_quality_tradeoff,
            constraints=constraints,
            user_id=req.user_id,
            namespace=req.namespace,
            allow_llm_escalation=req.allow_llm_escalation,
        )
        resp = await rec.recommend(sub)
        steps.append(StepRecommendation(step_id=step.step_id, recommendation=resp))
        total += resp.recommended_model.est_cost_usd
        premium += max(
            (m.est_cost_usd for m in resp.ranked),
            default=resp.recommended_model.est_cost_usd,
        )
        confidences.append(resp.confidence)

    confidence = sum(confidences) / len(confidences) if confidences else 0.0
    return WorkflowResponse(
        workflow_recommendation_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,
        steps=steps,
        total_est_cost_usd=round(total, 8),
        total_est_cost_if_all_premium=round(premium, 8),
        confidence=round(confidence, 4),
    )

1	"""The recommendation orchestrator."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import time
6	import uuid
7	
8	from minima.catalog.store import CatalogStore
9	from minima.config import Settings
10	from minima.llm.base import CandidateView, Reasoner
11	from minima.logging import get_logger
12	from minima.memory.adapter import Memory
13	from minima.memory.keys import build_content, salient_signature, task_cluster, task_fingerprint
14	from minima.memory.records import clamp01
15	from minima.recommender import escalation, score
16	from minima.recommender.aggregate import aggregate_by_model, apply_ipw
17	from minima.recommender.classify import classify
18	from minima.recommender.propensity import Propensity, PropensityTracker
19	from minima.recommender.recstore import RecStore, StoredRecommendation
20	from minima.recommender.types import CandidateScore, ModelAggregate
21	from minima.schemas.common import DecisionBasis, Difficulty, TaskType
22	from minima.schemas.models_catalog import ModelCard
23	from minima.schemas.recommend import (
24	    EvidenceRef,
25	    RankedModel,
26	    RecommendRequest,
27	    RecommendResponse,
28	)
29	
30	log = get_logger("minima.recommender")
31	
32	# Any positive recalled-outcome mass makes a candidate's prediction "memory-driven";
33	# the confidence field separately conveys how strong that evidence is.
34	MEMORY_WEIGHT_MIN = 0.0
35	# Max neighbors echoed back per candidate in the explained response.
36	MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CANDIDATE = 5
37	
38	
39	class NoCandidatesError(ValueError):
40	    """Raised when constraints eliminate every catalog model."""
41	
42	
43	class Recommender:
44	    def __init__(
45	        self,
46	        settings: Settings,
47	        memory: Memory,
48	        catalog_store: CatalogStore,
49	        recstore: RecStore,
50	        reasoner: Reasoner | None = None,
51	        propensity: Propensity | None = None,
52	    ):
53	        self._settings = settings
54	        self._memory = memory
55	        self._catalog_store = catalog_store
56	        self._recstore = recstore
57	        self._reasoner = reasoner
58	        self._propensity = propensity or PropensityTracker()
59	
60	    async def recommend(self, req: RecommendRequest) -> RecommendResponse:
61	        started = time.monotonic()
62	        settings = self._settings
63	        warnings: list[str] = []
64	
65	        task_type, difficulty = classify(req.task)
66	        task_type, difficulty = await self._maybe_llm_classify(req, task_type, difficulty, warnings)
67	        signature = (
68	            salient_signature(req.task.task, settings.minima_cluster_signature_tokens)
69	            if settings.minima_cluster_granularity.lower() == "fine"
70	            else None
71	        )
72	        cluster = task_cluster(task_type, difficulty, signature)
73	        fingerprint = task_fingerprint(req.task.task)
74	        lane = settings.lane(req.namespace)
75	        env_tags = req.task.tags or None
76	
77	        catalog = self._catalog_store.get()
78	        candidates = _select_candidates(catalog.cards, req, task_type, req.max_candidates)
79	        if not candidates:
80	            raise NoCandidatesError("no models match the supplied constraints")
81	        candidate_ids = {c.model_id for c in candidates}
82	
83	        recall = await self._memory.recall(
84	            query=req.task.task,
85	            lane=lane,
86	            user_id=req.user_id,
87	            limit=settings.minima_memory_recall_limit,
88	            env_tags=env_tags,
89	        )
90	        if recall.timed_out:
91	            warnings.append("recall_timeout")
92	        elif recall.error:
93	            warnings.append("memory_unavailable")
94	
95	        aggregates = aggregate_by_model(recall.outcome_evidence, candidate_ids)
96	        if settings.minima_ipw_enabled and aggregates:
97	            apply_ipw(
98	                aggregates,
99	                self._propensity.propensities(lane, cluster, candidate_ids),
100	                settings.minima_ipw_clip_low,
101	                settings.minima_ipw_clip_high,
102	            )
103	
104	        input_tokens = req.task.expected_input_tokens or settings.minima_default_input_tokens
105	        output_tokens = req.task.expected_output_tokens or settings.minima_default_output_tokens
106	        scored = self._score_candidates(
107	            candidates, aggregates, task_type, input_tokens, output_tokens, req
108	        )
109	
110	        if req.constraints.max_cost_per_call is not None:
111	            affordable = [c for c in scored if c.est_cost_usd <= req.constraints.max_cost_per_call]
112	            if affordable:
113	                scored = affordable
114	            else:
115	                warnings.append("no_model_within_cost_budget")
116	
117	        tau = score.threshold_from_slider(
118	            req.cost_quality_tradeoff,
119	            settings.minima_tau_min,
120	            settings.minima_tau_max,
121	            req.constraints.min_quality,
122	        )
123	
124	        recommended, fallback, ranked, opt_warnings = self._finalize(
125	            scored, tau, req.cost_quality_tradeoff
126	        )
127	        overall_basis = recommended.decision_basis
128	
129	        esc = escalation.evaluate(
130	            settings=settings,
131	            allow=req.allow_llm_escalation,
132	            total_weight=sum(a.weight_sum for a in aggregates.values()),
133	            distinct_models_with_evidence=sum(1 for a in aggregates.values() if a.weight_sum > 0),
134	            recommended_confidence=recommended.confidence,
135	            ranked=ranked,
136	            aggregates=aggregates,
137	        )
138	        if esc.should_escalate:
139	            warnings.extend(f"escalation_suggested:{reason}" for reason in esc.reasons)
140	            if self._reasoner is not None and settings.reasoner_enabled:
141	                consulted = await self._consult_reasoner(
142	                    scored=scored, task_type=task_type, difficulty=difficulty, lane=lane, req=req
143	                )
144	                if consulted:
145	                    recommended, fallback, ranked, opt_warnings = self._finalize(
146	                        scored, tau, req.cost_quality_tradeoff
147	                    )
148	                    overall_basis = DecisionBasis.llm
149	                    warnings.append("reasoner_consulted")
150	                else:
151	                    warnings.append("reasoner_failed")
152	            else:
153	                warnings.append("reasoner_disabled")
154	        warnings.extend(opt_warnings)
155	
156	        if not recall.outcome_evidence:
157	            warnings.append("cold_start")
158	        if catalog.stale:
159	            warnings.append("prices_stale")
160	
161	        self._propensity.record(lane, cluster, recommended.card.model_id)
162	
163	        recommendation_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
164	        self._recstore.put(
165	            StoredRecommendation(
166	                recommendation_id=recommendation_id,
167	                lane=lane,
168	                user_id=req.user_id,
169	                task_type=task_type.value,
170	                difficulty=difficulty.value,
171	                task_cluster=cluster,
172	                task_fingerprint=fingerprint,
173	                content=build_content(task_type.value, difficulty.value, req.task.task),
174	                env_tags=list(req.task.tags),
175	                recommended_model_id=recommended.card.model_id,
176	                neighbors_by_model={
177	                    mid: [(ev.entry_id, ev.reference_id) for ev in agg.evidence]
178	                    for mid, agg in aggregates.items()
179	                },
180	            )
181	        )
182	
183	        confidence = _overall_confidence(overall_basis, recommended.confidence)
184	        return RecommendResponse(
185	            recommendation_id=recommendation_id,
186	            recommended_model=_to_ranked_model(recommended, req.explain),
187	            ranked=[_to_ranked_model(c, req.explain) for c in ranked],
188	            fallback_model=_to_ranked_model(fallback, req.explain) if fallback else None,
189	            confidence=round(confidence, 4),
190	            decision_basis=overall_basis,
191	            threshold_used=round(tau, 4),
192	            classified_task_type=task_type,
193	            classified_difficulty=difficulty,
194	            catalog_version=catalog.version,
195	            catalog_stale=catalog.stale,
196	            latency_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
197	            warnings=warnings,
198	        )
199	
200	    async def _maybe_llm_classify(
201	        self,
202	        req: RecommendRequest,
203	        task_type: TaskType,
204	        difficulty: Difficulty,
205	        warnings: list[str],
206	    ) -> tuple[TaskType, Difficulty]:
207	        """Refine an ambiguous heuristic classification via the reasoner (best-effort)."""
208	        if not (
209	            self._settings.minima_reasoner_classify
210	            and req.allow_llm_escalation
211	            and req.task.task_type is None
212	            and task_type == TaskType.other
213	            and self._reasoner is not None
214	            and self._settings.reasoner_enabled
215	            and hasattr(self._reasoner, "classify")
216	        ):
217	            return task_type, difficulty
218	        try:
219	            result = await self._reasoner.classify(task=req.task.task)
220	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
221	            log.warning("llm_classify_failed", error=str(exc))
222	            return task_type, difficulty
223	        if result is None:
224	            return task_type, difficulty
225	        warnings.append("llm_classified")
226	        return result
227	
228	    async def _consult_reasoner(
229	        self,
230	        *,
231	        scored: list[CandidateScore],
232	        task_type: TaskType,
233	        difficulty: Difficulty,
234	        lane: str,
235	        req: RecommendRequest,
236	    ) -> bool:
237	        memory_block = await self._memory.get_context(
238	            query=req.task.task, lane=lane, user_id=req.user_id, max_token_budget=1500
239	        )
240	        views = [
241	            CandidateView(
242	                model_id=c.card.model_id,
243	                provider=c.card.provider,
244	                input_cost_per_mtok=c.card.input_cost_per_mtok,
245	                output_cost_per_mtok=c.card.output_cost_per_mtok,
246	                context_window=c.card.context_window,
247	                capability_prior=score.capability_prior(c.card, task_type),
248	                est_cost_usd=c.est_cost_usd,
249	                predicted_success=c.predicted_success,
250	            )
251	            for c in scored
252	        ]
253	        result = await self._reasoner.rank(  # type: ignore[union-attr]
254	            task=req.task.task,
255	            task_type=task_type.value,
256	            difficulty=difficulty.value,
257	            candidates=views,
258	            memory_block=memory_block,
259	            cost_quality_tradeoff=req.cost_quality_tradeoff,
260	        )
261	        if not result or not result.rankings:
262	            return False
263	        blend = self._settings.minima_reasoner_blend
264	        rankings = result.by_model()
265	        changed = False
266	        for c in scored:
267	            ranking = rankings.get(c.card.model_id)
268	            if ranking is None:
269	                continue
270	            c.predicted_success = clamp01(
271	                blend * ranking.predicted_success + (1.0 - blend) * c.predicted_success
272	            )
273	            c.decision_basis = DecisionBasis.llm
274	            if ranking.rationale:
275	                c.rationale = ranking.rationale
276	            changed = True
277	        return changed
278	
279	    def _finalize(
280	        self, scored: list[CandidateScore], tau: float, cost_quality_tradeoff: float
281	    ) -> tuple[CandidateScore, CandidateScore | None, list[CandidateScore], list[str]]:
282	        max_cost = max((c.est_cost_usd for c in scored), default=0.0) or 1.0
283	        for c in scored:
284	            c.score = score.ranking_score(
285	                c.predicted_success, c.est_cost_usd / max_cost, cost_quality_tradeoff
286	            )
287	        return _optimize(scored, tau)
288	
289	    def _score_candidates(
290	        self,
291	        candidates: list[ModelCard],
292	        aggregates: dict[str, ModelAggregate],
293	        task_type: TaskType,
294	        input_tokens: int,
295	        output_tokens: int,
296	        req: RecommendRequest,
297	    ) -> list[CandidateScore]:
298	        settings = self._settings
299	        scored: list[CandidateScore] = []
300	        min_cost_n = settings.minima_observed_cost_min_n
301	        # Decide the cost basis ONCE for the whole candidate set so all costs are compared
302	        # like-for-like (never mix per-request estimates with historical realized costs across
303	        # candidates). Prefers re-scaled observed output behavior (size-exact + reasoning-aware),
304	        # then robust observed $/call, else the cache-aware token estimate.
305	        cost_basis = score.choose_cost_basis(
306	            {c.model_id: aggregates.get(c.model_id) for c in candidates},
307	            settings.minima_use_observed_cost,
308	            req.constraints.require_prompt_caching,
309	            min_cost_n,
310	        )
311	        for card in candidates:
312	            agg = aggregates.get(card.model_id)
313	            prior = score.capability_prior(card, task_type)
314	            predicted, confidence = score.predicted_success(
315	                agg, prior, settings.minima_beta_pseudocount
316	            )
317	            predicted = score.with_exploration_bonus(
318	                predicted, confidence, settings.minima_exploration_bonus
319	            )
320	            use_cache = req.constraints.require_prompt_caching and card.supports_prompt_caching
321	            est_cost, breakdown = score.effective_cost(
322	                card, agg, input_tokens, output_tokens, use_cache, cost_basis, min_cost_n
323	            )
324	            cost_word = "obs" if ("observed_avg" in breakdown or "rescaled" in breakdown) else "est"
325	
326	            if agg is not None and agg.weight_sum > MEMORY_WEIGHT_MIN:
327	                basis = DecisionBasis.memory
328	                rationale = (
329	                    f"{agg.n} similar past outcome(s); weighted success "
330	                    f"{agg.weighted_success_rate:.0%}; {cost_word} ${est_cost:.5f}/call"
331	                )
332	                evidence = agg.evidence[:MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CANDIDATE]
333	            else:
334	                basis = DecisionBasis.prior
335	                rationale = (
336	                    f"no memory yet; capability prior {prior:.0%} for {task_type.value}; "
337	                    f"{cost_word} ${est_cost:.5f}/call"
338	                )
339	                evidence = agg.evidence[:MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CANDIDATE] if agg else []
340	
341	            scored.append(
342	                CandidateScore(
343	                    card=card,
344	                    predicted_success=predicted,
345	                    confidence=confidence,
346	                    est_cost_usd=est_cost,
347	                    est_cost_breakdown=breakdown,
348	                    decision_basis=basis,
349	                    evidence=evidence,
350	                    rationale=rationale,
351	                )
352	            )
353	        return scored
354	
355	
356	def _overall_confidence(basis: DecisionBasis, recommended_confidence: float) -> float:
357	    if basis == DecisionBasis.memory:
358	        return recommended_confidence
359	    if basis == DecisionBasis.llm:
360	        return max(recommended_confidence, 0.5)
361	    return min(recommended_confidence, 0.5)
362	
363	
364	def _select_candidates(
365	    cards: list[ModelCard], req: RecommendRequest, task_type: TaskType, max_candidates: int
366	) -> list[ModelCard]:
367	    c = req.constraints
368	    selected = list(cards)
369	    if c.candidate_models:
370	        wanted = set(c.candidate_models)
371	        selected = [m for m in selected if m.model_id in wanted]
372	    if c.allowed_providers:
373	        allowed = {p.lower() for p in c.allowed_providers}
374	        selected = [m for m in selected if m.provider.lower() in allowed]
375	    if c.excluded_models:
376	        excluded = set(c.excluded_models)
377	        selected = [m for m in selected if m.model_id not in excluded]
378	    if c.require_prompt_caching:
379	        selected = [m for m in selected if m.supports_prompt_caching]
380	    if c.require_context_window:
381	        selected = [m for m in selected if m.context_window >= c.require_context_window]
382	    selected.sort(key=lambda m: score.capability_prior(m, task_type), reverse=True)
383	    return selected[:max_candidates]
384	
385	
386	def _optimize(
387	    scored: list[CandidateScore], tau: float
388	) -> tuple[CandidateScore, CandidateScore | None, list[CandidateScore], list[str]]:
389	    warnings: list[str] = []
390	    ranked = sorted(scored, key=lambda c: c.score, reverse=True)
391	    eligible = [c for c in scored if c.predicted_success >= tau]
392	
393	    if eligible:
394	        recommended = min(
395	            eligible, key=lambda c: (c.est_cost_usd, -c.predicted_success, -c.confidence)
396	        )
397	    else:
398	        warnings.append("no_model_meets_threshold")
399	        recommended = max(scored, key=lambda c: c.predicted_success)
400	
401	    others = [c for c in eligible if c.card.model_id != recommended.card.model_id]
402	    reliable = [c for c in others if c.predicted_success >= tau + 0.05]
403	    if reliable:
404	        fallback: CandidateScore | None = min(reliable, key=lambda c: c.est_cost_usd)
405	    else:
406	        rest = [c for c in ranked if c.card.model_id != recommended.card.model_id]
407	        fallback = max(rest, key=lambda c: c.predicted_success) if rest else None
408	
409	    return recommended, fallback, ranked, warnings
410	
411	
412	def _to_ranked_model(c: CandidateScore, explain: bool) -> RankedModel:
413	    evidence = (
414	        [
415	            EvidenceRef(
416	                entry_id=ev.entry_id,
417	                reference_id=ev.reference_id,
418	                model_id=ev.record.model_id if ev.record else c.card.model_id,
419	                score=round(ev.score, 4),
420	                knowledge_confidence=round(ev.knowledge_confidence, 4),
421	                observed_success=round(ev.record.quality_score, 4) if ev.record else 0.0,
422	                is_stale=ev.is_stale,
423	            )
424	            for ev in c.evidence
425	        ]
426	        if explain
427	        else []
428	    )
429	    return RankedModel(
430	        model_id=c.card.model_id,
431	        provider=c.card.provider,
432	        predicted_success=round(c.predicted_success, 4),
433	        est_cost_usd=round(c.est_cost_usd, 8),
434	        est_cost_breakdown=c.est_cost_breakdown,
435	        score=round(c.score, 4),
436	        rationale=c.rationale,
437	        decision_basis=c.decision_basis,
438	        evidence=evidence,
439	        supports_prompt_caching=c.card.supports_prompt_caching,
440	        context_window=c.card.context_window,
441	    )
442

"""The recommendation orchestrator."""

from __future__ import annotations

import time
import uuid

from minima.catalog.store import CatalogStore
from minima.config import Settings
from minima.llm.base import CandidateView, Reasoner
from minima.logging import get_logger
from minima.memory.adapter import Memory
from minima.memory.keys import build_content, salient_signature, task_cluster, task_fingerprint
from minima.memory.records import clamp01
from minima.recommender import escalation, score
from minima.recommender.aggregate import aggregate_by_model, apply_ipw
from minima.recommender.classify import classify
from minima.recommender.propensity import Propensity, PropensityTracker
from minima.recommender.recstore import RecStore, StoredRecommendation
from minima.recommender.types import CandidateScore, ModelAggregate
from minima.schemas.common import DecisionBasis, Difficulty, TaskType
from minima.schemas.models_catalog import ModelCard
from minima.schemas.recommend import (
    EvidenceRef,
    RankedModel,
    RecommendRequest,
    RecommendResponse,
)

log = get_logger("minima.recommender")

# Any positive recalled-outcome mass makes a candidate's prediction "memory-driven";
# the confidence field separately conveys how strong that evidence is.
MEMORY_WEIGHT_MIN = 0.0
# Max neighbors echoed back per candidate in the explained response.
MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CANDIDATE = 5


class NoCandidatesError(ValueError):
    """Raised when constraints eliminate every catalog model."""


class Recommender:
    def __init__(
        self,
        settings: Settings,
        memory: Memory,
        catalog_store: CatalogStore,
        recstore: RecStore,
        reasoner: Reasoner | None = None,
        propensity: Propensity | None = None,
    ):
        self._settings = settings
        self._memory = memory
        self._catalog_store = catalog_store
        self._recstore = recstore
        self._reasoner = reasoner
        self._propensity = propensity or PropensityTracker()

    async def recommend(self, req: RecommendRequest) -> RecommendResponse:
        started = time.monotonic()
        settings = self._settings
        warnings: list[str] = []

        task_type, difficulty = classify(req.task)
        task_type, difficulty = await self._maybe_llm_classify(req, task_type, difficulty, warnings)
        signature = (
            salient_signature(req.task.task, settings.minima_cluster_signature_tokens)
            if settings.minima_cluster_granularity.lower() == "fine"
            else None
        )
        cluster = task_cluster(task_type, difficulty, signature)
        fingerprint = task_fingerprint(req.task.task)
        lane = settings.lane(req.namespace)
        env_tags = req.task.tags or None

        catalog = self._catalog_store.get()
        candidates = _select_candidates(catalog.cards, req, task_type, req.max_candidates)
        if not candidates:
            raise NoCandidatesError("no models match the supplied constraints")
        candidate_ids = {c.model_id for c in candidates}

        recall = await self._memory.recall(
            query=req.task.task,
            lane=lane,
            user_id=req.user_id,
            limit=settings.minima_memory_recall_limit,
            env_tags=env_tags,
        )
        if recall.timed_out:
            warnings.append("recall_timeout")
        elif recall.error:
            warnings.append("memory_unavailable")

        aggregates = aggregate_by_model(recall.outcome_evidence, candidate_ids)
        if settings.minima_ipw_enabled and aggregates:
            apply_ipw(
                aggregates,
                self._propensity.propensities(lane, cluster, candidate_ids),
                settings.minima_ipw_clip_low,
                settings.minima_ipw_clip_high,
            )

        input_tokens = req.task.expected_input_tokens or settings.minima_default_input_tokens
        output_tokens = req.task.expected_output_tokens or settings.minima_default_output_tokens
        scored = self._score_candidates(
            candidates, aggregates, task_type, input_tokens, output_tokens, req
        )

        if req.constraints.max_cost_per_call is not None:
            affordable = [c for c in scored if c.est_cost_usd <= req.constraints.max_cost_per_call]
            if affordable:
                scored = affordable
            else:
                warnings.append("no_model_within_cost_budget")

        tau = score.threshold_from_slider(
            req.cost_quality_tradeoff,
            settings.minima_tau_min,
            settings.minima_tau_max,
            req.constraints.min_quality,
        )

        recommended, fallback, ranked, opt_warnings = self._finalize(
            scored, tau, req.cost_quality_tradeoff
        )
        overall_basis = recommended.decision_basis

        esc = escalation.evaluate(
            settings=settings,
            allow=req.allow_llm_escalation,
            total_weight=sum(a.weight_sum for a in aggregates.values()),
            distinct_models_with_evidence=sum(1 for a in aggregates.values() if a.weight_sum > 0),
            recommended_confidence=recommended.confidence,
            ranked=ranked,
            aggregates=aggregates,
        )
        if esc.should_escalate:
            warnings.extend(f"escalation_suggested:{reason}" for reason in esc.reasons)
            if self._reasoner is not None and settings.reasoner_enabled:
                consulted = await self._consult_reasoner(
                    scored=scored, task_type=task_type, difficulty=difficulty, lane=lane, req=req
                )
                if consulted:
                    recommended, fallback, ranked, opt_warnings = self._finalize(
                        scored, tau, req.cost_quality_tradeoff
                    )
                    overall_basis = DecisionBasis.llm
                    warnings.append("reasoner_consulted")
                else:
                    warnings.append("reasoner_failed")
            else:
                warnings.append("reasoner_disabled")
        warnings.extend(opt_warnings)

        if not recall.outcome_evidence:
            warnings.append("cold_start")
        if catalog.stale:
            warnings.append("prices_stale")

        self._propensity.record(lane, cluster, recommended.card.model_id)

        recommendation_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
        self._recstore.put(
            StoredRecommendation(
                recommendation_id=recommendation_id,
                lane=lane,
                user_id=req.user_id,
                task_type=task_type.value,
                difficulty=difficulty.value,
                task_cluster=cluster,
                task_fingerprint=fingerprint,
                content=build_content(task_type.value, difficulty.value, req.task.task),
                env_tags=list(req.task.tags),
                recommended_model_id=recommended.card.model_id,
                neighbors_by_model={
                    mid: [(ev.entry_id, ev.reference_id) for ev in agg.evidence]
                    for mid, agg in aggregates.items()
                },
            )
        )

        confidence = _overall_confidence(overall_basis, recommended.confidence)
        return RecommendResponse(
            recommendation_id=recommendation_id,
            recommended_model=_to_ranked_model(recommended, req.explain),
            ranked=[_to_ranked_model(c, req.explain) for c in ranked],
            fallback_model=_to_ranked_model(fallback, req.explain) if fallback else None,
            confidence=round(confidence, 4),
            decision_basis=overall_basis,
            threshold_used=round(tau, 4),
            classified_task_type=task_type,
            classified_difficulty=difficulty,
            catalog_version=catalog.version,
            catalog_stale=catalog.stale,
            latency_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
            warnings=warnings,
        )

    async def _maybe_llm_classify(
        self,
        req: RecommendRequest,
        task_type: TaskType,
        difficulty: Difficulty,
        warnings: list[str],
    ) -> tuple[TaskType, Difficulty]:
        """Refine an ambiguous heuristic classification via the reasoner (best-effort)."""
        if not (
            self._settings.minima_reasoner_classify
            and req.allow_llm_escalation
            and req.task.task_type is None
            and task_type == TaskType.other
            and self._reasoner is not None
            and self._settings.reasoner_enabled
            and hasattr(self._reasoner, "classify")
        ):
            return task_type, difficulty
        try:
            result = await self._reasoner.classify(task=req.task.task)
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            log.warning("llm_classify_failed", error=str(exc))
            return task_type, difficulty
        if result is None:
            return task_type, difficulty
        warnings.append("llm_classified")
        return result

    async def _consult_reasoner(
        self,
        *,
        scored: list[CandidateScore],
        task_type: TaskType,
        difficulty: Difficulty,
        lane: str,
        req: RecommendRequest,
    ) -> bool:
        memory_block = await self._memory.get_context(
            query=req.task.task, lane=lane, user_id=req.user_id, max_token_budget=1500
        )
        views = [
            CandidateView(
                model_id=c.card.model_id,
                provider=c.card.provider,
                input_cost_per_mtok=c.card.input_cost_per_mtok,
                output_cost_per_mtok=c.card.output_cost_per_mtok,
                context_window=c.card.context_window,
                capability_prior=score.capability_prior(c.card, task_type),
                est_cost_usd=c.est_cost_usd,
                predicted_success=c.predicted_success,
            )
            for c in scored
        ]
        result = await self._reasoner.rank(  # type: ignore[union-attr]
            task=req.task.task,
            task_type=task_type.value,
            difficulty=difficulty.value,
            candidates=views,
            memory_block=memory_block,
            cost_quality_tradeoff=req.cost_quality_tradeoff,
        )
        if not result or not result.rankings:
            return False
        blend = self._settings.minima_reasoner_blend
        rankings = result.by_model()
        changed = False
        for c in scored:
            ranking = rankings.get(c.card.model_id)
            if ranking is None:
                continue
            c.predicted_success = clamp01(
                blend * ranking.predicted_success + (1.0 - blend) * c.predicted_success
            )
            c.decision_basis = DecisionBasis.llm
            if ranking.rationale:
                c.rationale = ranking.rationale
            changed = True
        return changed

    def _finalize(
        self, scored: list[CandidateScore], tau: float, cost_quality_tradeoff: float
    ) -> tuple[CandidateScore, CandidateScore | None, list[CandidateScore], list[str]]:
        max_cost = max((c.est_cost_usd for c in scored), default=0.0) or 1.0
        for c in scored:
            c.score = score.ranking_score(
                c.predicted_success, c.est_cost_usd / max_cost, cost_quality_tradeoff
            )
        return _optimize(scored, tau)

    def _score_candidates(
        self,
        candidates: list[ModelCard],
        aggregates: dict[str, ModelAggregate],
        task_type: TaskType,
        input_tokens: int,
        output_tokens: int,
        req: RecommendRequest,
    ) -> list[CandidateScore]:
        settings = self._settings
        scored: list[CandidateScore] = []
        min_cost_n = settings.minima_observed_cost_min_n
        # Decide the cost basis ONCE for the whole candidate set so all costs are compared
        # like-for-like (never mix per-request estimates with historical realized costs across
        # candidates). Prefers re-scaled observed output behavior (size-exact + reasoning-aware),
        # then robust observed $/call, else the cache-aware token estimate.
        cost_basis = score.choose_cost_basis(
            {c.model_id: aggregates.get(c.model_id) for c in candidates},
            settings.minima_use_observed_cost,
            req.constraints.require_prompt_caching,
            min_cost_n,
        )
        for card in candidates:
            agg = aggregates.get(card.model_id)
            prior = score.capability_prior(card, task_type)
            predicted, confidence = score.predicted_success(
                agg, prior, settings.minima_beta_pseudocount
            )
            predicted = score.with_exploration_bonus(
                predicted, confidence, settings.minima_exploration_bonus
            )
            use_cache = req.constraints.require_prompt_caching and card.supports_prompt_caching
            est_cost, breakdown = score.effective_cost(
                card, agg, input_tokens, output_tokens, use_cache, cost_basis, min_cost_n
            )
            cost_word = "obs" if ("observed_avg" in breakdown or "rescaled" in breakdown) else "est"

            if agg is not None and agg.weight_sum > MEMORY_WEIGHT_MIN:
                basis = DecisionBasis.memory
                rationale = (
                    f"{agg.n} similar past outcome(s); weighted success "
                    f"{agg.weighted_success_rate:.0%}; {cost_word} ${est_cost:.5f}/call"
                )
                evidence = agg.evidence[:MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CANDIDATE]
            else:
                basis = DecisionBasis.prior
                rationale = (
                    f"no memory yet; capability prior {prior:.0%} for {task_type.value}; "
                    f"{cost_word} ${est_cost:.5f}/call"
                )
                evidence = agg.evidence[:MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CANDIDATE] if agg else []

            scored.append(
                CandidateScore(
                    card=card,
                    predicted_success=predicted,
                    confidence=confidence,
                    est_cost_usd=est_cost,
                    est_cost_breakdown=breakdown,
                    decision_basis=basis,
                    evidence=evidence,
                    rationale=rationale,
                )
            )
        return scored


def _overall_confidence(basis: DecisionBasis, recommended_confidence: float) -> float:
    if basis == DecisionBasis.memory:
        return recommended_confidence
    if basis == DecisionBasis.llm:
        return max(recommended_confidence, 0.5)
    return min(recommended_confidence, 0.5)


def _select_candidates(
    cards: list[ModelCard], req: RecommendRequest, task_type: TaskType, max_candidates: int
) -> list[ModelCard]:
    c = req.constraints
    selected = list(cards)
    if c.candidate_models:
        wanted = set(c.candidate_models)
        selected = [m for m in selected if m.model_id in wanted]
    if c.allowed_providers:
        allowed = {p.lower() for p in c.allowed_providers}
        selected = [m for m in selected if m.provider.lower() in allowed]
    if c.excluded_models:
        excluded = set(c.excluded_models)
        selected = [m for m in selected if m.model_id not in excluded]
    if c.require_prompt_caching:
        selected = [m for m in selected if m.supports_prompt_caching]
    if c.require_context_window:
        selected = [m for m in selected if m.context_window >= c.require_context_window]
    selected.sort(key=lambda m: score.capability_prior(m, task_type), reverse=True)
    return selected[:max_candidates]


def _optimize(
    scored: list[CandidateScore], tau: float
) -> tuple[CandidateScore, CandidateScore | None, list[CandidateScore], list[str]]:
    warnings: list[str] = []
    ranked = sorted(scored, key=lambda c: c.score, reverse=True)
    eligible = [c for c in scored if c.predicted_success >= tau]

    if eligible:
        recommended = min(
            eligible, key=lambda c: (c.est_cost_usd, -c.predicted_success, -c.confidence)
        )
    else:
        warnings.append("no_model_meets_threshold")
        recommended = max(scored, key=lambda c: c.predicted_success)

    others = [c for c in eligible if c.card.model_id != recommended.card.model_id]
    reliable = [c for c in others if c.predicted_success >= tau + 0.05]
    if reliable:
        fallback: CandidateScore | None = min(reliable, key=lambda c: c.est_cost_usd)
    else:
        rest = [c for c in ranked if c.card.model_id != recommended.card.model_id]
        fallback = max(rest, key=lambda c: c.predicted_success) if rest else None

    return recommended, fallback, ranked, warnings


def _to_ranked_model(c: CandidateScore, explain: bool) -> RankedModel:
    evidence = (
        [
            EvidenceRef(
                entry_id=ev.entry_id,
                reference_id=ev.reference_id,
                model_id=ev.record.model_id if ev.record else c.card.model_id,
                score=round(ev.score, 4),
                knowledge_confidence=round(ev.knowledge_confidence, 4),
                observed_success=round(ev.record.quality_score, 4) if ev.record else 0.0,
                is_stale=ev.is_stale,
            )
            for ev in c.evidence
        ]
        if explain
        else []
    )
    return RankedModel(
        model_id=c.card.model_id,
        provider=c.card.provider,
        predicted_success=round(c.predicted_success, 4),
        est_cost_usd=round(c.est_cost_usd, 8),
        est_cost_breakdown=c.est_cost_breakdown,
        score=round(c.score, 4),
        rationale=c.rationale,
        decision_basis=c.decision_basis,
        evidence=evidence,
        supports_prompt_caching=c.card.supports_prompt_caching,
        context_window=c.card.context_window,
    )

26	router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1", tags=["feedback"])

29	def _fire_reflect(memory: Memory, lane: str, user_id: str | None) -> None:
30	    async def _run() -> None:
31	        try:
32	            await memory.reflect(lane=lane, user_id=user_id)
33	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
34	            log.warning("reflect_failed", lane=lane, error=str(exc))

40	async def feedback(
41	    req: FeedbackRequest,
42	    tenant: TenantContext = Depends(get_tenant),
43	    settings: Settings = Depends(get_settings),
44	) -> FeedbackResponse:
45	    memory = tenant.memory
46	    # Org-scoped store: a recommendation_id minted for another org resolves to None here,
47	    # so org A cannot credit or poison org B's recommendation.
48	    stored = tenant.recstore.get(req.recommendation_id)
49	    if stored is None:
50	        return FeedbackResponse(accepted=False, warnings=["unknown_recommendation"])
51	
52	    quality = quality_from_outcome(req.outcome.value, req.quality_score)
53	    signal = signal_from_outcome(req.outcome.value, quality)
54	    is_success = req.outcome == OutcomeLabel.success
55	
56	    record = OutcomeRecord(
57	        model_id=req.chosen_model_id,
58	        task_type=stored.task_type,
59	        difficulty=stored.difficulty,
60	        task_fingerprint=stored.task_fingerprint,
61	        task_cluster=stored.task_cluster,
62	        input_tokens=req.input_tokens or 0,
63	        output_tokens=req.output_tokens or 0,
64	        cost_usd=req.actual_cost_usd or 0.0,
65	        latency_ms=req.latency_ms,
66	        quality_score=quality,
67	        outcome=req.outcome.value,
68	        recommendation_id=req.recommendation_id,
69	        verified_in_production=req.verified_in_production,
70	    )
71	    upsert_key = outcome_upsert_key(stored.task_cluster, req.chosen_model_id)
72	    idem = req.idempotency_key or outcome_idempotency_key(
73	        req.recommendation_id, req.chosen_model_id
74	    )
75	    importance = "high" if (req.verified_in_production and is_success) else "medium"
76	    warnings: list[str] = []
77	
78	    try:
79	        record_id = await memory.remember_outcome(
80	            content=stored.content,
81	            record=record,
82	            lane=stored.lane,
83	            upsert_key=upsert_key,
84	            idempotency_key=idem,
85	            user_id=stored.user_id,
86	            env_tags=stored.env_tags or None,
87	            importance=importance,
88	            source="human",
89	        )
90	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
91	        log.warning("remember_outcome_failed", error=str(exc))
92	        return FeedbackResponse(accepted=False, warnings=["memory_write_failed"])
93	
94	    neighbors = stored.neighbors_by_model.get(req.chosen_model_id, [])
95	    entry_ids = [eid for (eid, _ref) in neighbors if eid]
96	    primary_ref = next((ref for (_eid, ref) in neighbors if ref), None) or record_id
97	
98	    updated_confidence: float | None = None
99	    if primary_ref:
100	        try:
101	            oc = await memory.record_outcome(
102	                lane=stored.lane,
103	                reference_id=primary_ref,
104	                outcome=req.outcome.value,
105	                signal=signal,
106	                entry_ids=entry_ids or None,
107	                user_id=stored.user_id,
108	                verified_in_production=req.verified_in_production,
109	                idempotency_key=f"oc:{idem}",
110	                rationale=f"minima feedback {req.recommendation_id}: ran {req.chosen_model_id}",
111	            )
112	            value = oc.get("updated_confidence")
113	            updated_confidence = float(value) if value is not None else None
114	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
115	            log.warning("record_outcome_failed", error=str(exc))
116	            warnings.append("reinforcement_failed")
117	
118	    # Promote a verified-in-production strong success to a durable Lesson. Lessons pass
119	    # the server's validation gate and feed reflect()/surface_strategies rule promotion;
120	    # a per-(cluster, model) upsert_key keeps one accumulating lesson instead of flooding.
121	    lesson_promoted = False
122	    if (
123	        settings.minima_lesson_on_verified_prod
124	        and req.verified_in_production
125	        and is_success
126	        and quality >= settings.minima_lesson_min_quality
127	    ):
128	        try:
129	            await memory.remember_lesson(
130	                content=build_lesson_content(stored.task_cluster, req.chosen_model_id, quality),
131	                lane=stored.lane,
132	                upsert_key=lesson_upsert_key(stored.task_cluster, req.chosen_model_id),
133	                user_id=stored.user_id,
134	                env_tags=stored.env_tags or None,
135	                metadata={
136	                    "kind": "lesson",
137	                    "task_cluster": stored.task_cluster,
138	                    "model_id": req.chosen_model_id,
139	                    "verified_in_production": True,
140	                },
141	                idempotency_key=f"lsn:{idem}",
142	            )
143	            lesson_promoted = True
144	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — lesson promotion is best-effort
145	            log.warning("lesson_promotion_failed", error=str(exc))
146	            warnings.append("lesson_promotion_failed")
147	
148	    reflection_triggered = False
149	    count = tenant.lane_counter.bump(tenant.counter_key(stored.lane))
150	    every = settings.minima_reflect_every_n
151	    if (every > 0 and count % every == 0) or (req.verified_in_production and not is_success):
152	        _fire_reflect(memory, stored.lane, stored.user_id)
153	        reflection_triggered = True
154	
155	    return FeedbackResponse(
156	        accepted=True,
157	        record_id=record_id,
158	        reinforced_entry_ids=entry_ids,
159	        updated_confidence=updated_confidence,
160	        reflection_triggered=reflection_triggered,
161	        lesson_promoted=lesson_promoted,
162	        warnings=warnings,
163	    )

"""Feedback endpoint — writes the outcome to Mubit and closes the learning loop."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from minima.api.auth import get_tenant
from minima.config import Settings
from minima.deps import get_settings
from minima.logging import get_logger
from minima.memory.adapter import Memory
from minima.memory.keys import (
    build_lesson_content,
    lesson_upsert_key,
    outcome_idempotency_key,
    outcome_upsert_key,
)
from minima.memory.records import OutcomeRecord, quality_from_outcome, signal_from_outcome
from minima.schemas.common import OutcomeLabel
from minima.schemas.feedback import FeedbackRequest, FeedbackResponse
from minima.tenancy.context import TenantContext

log = get_logger("minima.feedback")
router = APIRouter(prefix="/v1", tags=["feedback"])


def _fire_reflect(memory: Memory, lane: str, user_id: str | None) -> None:
    async def _run() -> None:
        try:
            await memory.reflect(lane=lane, user_id=user_id)
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            log.warning("reflect_failed", lane=lane, error=str(exc))

    asyncio.create_task(_run())  # noqa: RUF006 — fire-and-forget, errors are logged


@router.post("/feedback", response_model=FeedbackResponse)
async def feedback(
    req: FeedbackRequest,
    tenant: TenantContext = Depends(get_tenant),
    settings: Settings = Depends(get_settings),
) -> FeedbackResponse:
    memory = tenant.memory
    # Org-scoped store: a recommendation_id minted for another org resolves to None here,
    # so org A cannot credit or poison org B's recommendation.
    stored = tenant.recstore.get(req.recommendation_id)
    if stored is None:
        return FeedbackResponse(accepted=False, warnings=["unknown_recommendation"])

    quality = quality_from_outcome(req.outcome.value, req.quality_score)
    signal = signal_from_outcome(req.outcome.value, quality)
    is_success = req.outcome == OutcomeLabel.success

    record = OutcomeRecord(
        model_id=req.chosen_model_id,
        task_type=stored.task_type,
        difficulty=stored.difficulty,
        task_fingerprint=stored.task_fingerprint,
        task_cluster=stored.task_cluster,
        input_tokens=req.input_tokens or 0,
        output_tokens=req.output_tokens or 0,
        cost_usd=req.actual_cost_usd or 0.0,
        latency_ms=req.latency_ms,
        quality_score=quality,
        outcome=req.outcome.value,
        recommendation_id=req.recommendation_id,
        verified_in_production=req.verified_in_production,
    )
    upsert_key = outcome_upsert_key(stored.task_cluster, req.chosen_model_id)
    idem = req.idempotency_key or outcome_idempotency_key(
        req.recommendation_id, req.chosen_model_id
    )
    importance = "high" if (req.verified_in_production and is_success) else "medium"
    warnings: list[str] = []

    try:
        record_id = await memory.remember_outcome(
            content=stored.content,
            record=record,
            lane=stored.lane,
            upsert_key=upsert_key,
            idempotency_key=idem,
            user_id=stored.user_id,
            env_tags=stored.env_tags or None,
            importance=importance,
            source="human",
        )
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        log.warning("remember_outcome_failed", error=str(exc))
        return FeedbackResponse(accepted=False, warnings=["memory_write_failed"])

    neighbors = stored.neighbors_by_model.get(req.chosen_model_id, [])
    entry_ids = [eid for (eid, _ref) in neighbors if eid]
    primary_ref = next((ref for (_eid, ref) in neighbors if ref), None) or record_id

    updated_confidence: float | None = None
    if primary_ref:
        try:
            oc = await memory.record_outcome(
                lane=stored.lane,
                reference_id=primary_ref,
                outcome=req.outcome.value,
                signal=signal,
                entry_ids=entry_ids or None,
                user_id=stored.user_id,
                verified_in_production=req.verified_in_production,
                idempotency_key=f"oc:{idem}",
                rationale=f"minima feedback {req.recommendation_id}: ran {req.chosen_model_id}",
            )
            value = oc.get("updated_confidence")
            updated_confidence = float(value) if value is not None else None
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            log.warning("record_outcome_failed", error=str(exc))
            warnings.append("reinforcement_failed")

    # Promote a verified-in-production strong success to a durable Lesson. Lessons pass
    # the server's validation gate and feed reflect()/surface_strategies rule promotion;
    # a per-(cluster, model) upsert_key keeps one accumulating lesson instead of flooding.
    lesson_promoted = False
    if (
        settings.minima_lesson_on_verified_prod
        and req.verified_in_production
        and is_success
        and quality >= settings.minima_lesson_min_quality
    ):
        try:
            await memory.remember_lesson(
                content=build_lesson_content(stored.task_cluster, req.chosen_model_id, quality),
                lane=stored.lane,
                upsert_key=lesson_upsert_key(stored.task_cluster, req.chosen_model_id),
                user_id=stored.user_id,
                env_tags=stored.env_tags or None,
                metadata={
                    "kind": "lesson",
                    "task_cluster": stored.task_cluster,
                    "model_id": req.chosen_model_id,
                    "verified_in_production": True,
                },
                idempotency_key=f"lsn:{idem}",
            )
            lesson_promoted = True
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — lesson promotion is best-effort
            log.warning("lesson_promotion_failed", error=str(exc))
            warnings.append("lesson_promotion_failed")

    reflection_triggered = False
    count = tenant.lane_counter.bump(tenant.counter_key(stored.lane))
    every = settings.minima_reflect_every_n
    if (every > 0 and count % every == 0) or (req.verified_in_production and not is_success):
        _fire_reflect(memory, stored.lane, stored.user_id)
        reflection_triggered = True

    return FeedbackResponse(
        accepted=True,
        record_id=record_id,
        reinforced_entry_ids=entry_ids,
        updated_confidence=updated_confidence,
        reflection_triggered=reflection_triggered,
        lesson_promoted=lesson_promoted,
        warnings=warnings,
    )

1	"""The single integration point with the Mubit SDK.
2	
3	Everything Minima knows about Mubit lives here. The recommender depends only on the
4	``Memory`` protocol, so tests can swap in a fake. All SDK calls are synchronous and
5	run in a worker thread; the recall path is latency-bounded.
6	
7	Mubit run scoping: Minima uses the memory *lane* string as the run_id, so a namespace
8	maps to one stable run. That keeps ``upsert_key`` (scoped to run_id + user_id) stable
9	across requests, and recall over the same run finds the accumulated outcomes.
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	from collections.abc import Mapping, Sequence
15	from typing import Any, Protocol, runtime_checkable
16	
17	import anyio
18	import httpx
19	from mubit import Client, TransportError
20	
21	from minima.config import Settings
22	from minima.logging import get_logger
23	from minima.memory import threadpool
24	from minima.memory.records import OutcomeRecord, RecalledEvidence, RecallResult
25	
26	log = get_logger("minima.memory")
27	
28	# Lowercase LTM entry-type tags for Mubit's query filter. Recall deliberately does
29	# NOT filter by type (seeds land as "fact", feedback as "observation"); Minima outcomes
30	# are selected by metadata kind instead. Used by get_context only.
31	CONTEXT_ENTRY_TYPES = ["observation", "lesson", "fact"]
32	
33	
34	def _f(value: object, default: float = 0.0) -> float:
35	    try:
36	        return float(value)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
37	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
38	        return default
39	
40	
41	@runtime_checkable
42	class Memory(Protocol):
43	    async def recall(
44	        self,
45	        *,
46	        query: str,
47	        lane: str,
48	        user_id: str | None = None,
49	        limit: int = 25,
50	        entry_types: Sequence[str] | None = None,
51	        env_tags: Sequence[str] | None = None,
52	        timeout_ms: int | None = None,
53	    ) -> RecallResult: ...
54	
55	    async def remember_outcome(
56	        self,
57	        *,
58	        content: str,
59	        record: OutcomeRecord,
60	        lane: str,
61	        upsert_key: str,
62	        idempotency_key: str,
63	        user_id: str | None = None,
64	        env_tags: Sequence[str] | None = None,
65	        importance: str = "medium",
66	        source: str = "human",
67	    ) -> str | None: ...
68	
69	    async def record_outcome(
70	        self,
71	        *,
72	        lane: str,
73	        reference_id: str,
74	        outcome: str,
75	        signal: float,
76	        entry_ids: Sequence[str] | None = None,
77	        user_id: str | None = None,
78	        verified_in_production: bool = False,
79	        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
80	        rationale: str = "",
81	    ) -> dict: ...
82	
83	    async def remember_lesson(
84	        self,
85	        *,
86	        content: str,
87	        lane: str,
88	        upsert_key: str,
89	        user_id: str | None = None,
90	        lesson_type: str = "success",
91	        lesson_scope: str = "session",
92	        importance: str = "high",
93	        env_tags: Sequence[str] | None = None,
94	        metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
95	        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
96	    ) -> str | None: ...
97	
98	    async def batch_insert(
99	        self, *, run_id: str, items: list[dict], deduplicate: bool = True
100	    ) -> dict: ...
101	
102	    async def get_context(
103	        self,
104	        *,
105	        query: str,
106	        lane: str,
107	        user_id: str | None = None,
108	        entry_types: Sequence[str] | None = None,
109	        max_token_budget: int = 1500,
110	    ) -> str: ...
111	
112	    async def reflect(
113	        self, *, lane: str, user_id: str | None = None, include_linked_runs: bool = False
114	    ) -> dict: ...
115	
116	    async def surface_strategies(
117	        self, *, lane: str, lesson_types: Sequence[str] | None = None, max_strategies: int = 5
118	    ) -> dict: ...
119	
120	    async def health(self) -> dict: ...
121	
122	
123	class MubitMemory:
124	    """Concrete ``Memory`` backed by the Mubit SDK Client."""
125	
126	    def __init__(
127	        self,
128	        settings: Settings,
129	        *,
130	        endpoint: str | None = None,
131	        api_key: str | None = None,
132	        transport: str | None = None,
133	    ):
134	        # endpoint/api_key/transport override settings so one process can hold a distinct
135	        # client per org (multi-tenancy). They default to the single-tenant env config.
136	        self._settings = settings
137	        self._endpoint = endpoint or settings.mubit_endpoint
138	        self._transport = transport or settings.mubit_transport
139	        resolved_key = api_key if api_key is not None else settings.mubit_api_key
140	        kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
141	            "endpoint": self._endpoint,
142	            "timeout_ms": settings.mubit_timeout_ms,
143	        }
144	        if resolved_key:
145	            kwargs["api_key"] = resolved_key
146	        if self._transport:
147	            kwargs["transport"] = self._transport
148	        self._client = Client(**kwargs)
149	
150	    # ---- reads -----------------------------------------------------------------
151	
152	    async def recall(
153	        self,
154	        *,
155	        query: str,
156	        lane: str,
157	        user_id: str | None = None,
158	        limit: int = 25,
159	        entry_types: Sequence[str] | None = None,
160	        env_tags: Sequence[str] | None = None,
161	        timeout_ms: int | None = None,
162	    ) -> RecallResult:
163	        budget_ms = (
164	            timeout_ms if timeout_ms is not None else self._settings.minima_memory_recall_timeout_ms
165	        )
166	        # No entry_type filter by default: seed records (batch_insert) land as
167	        # "fact" while feedback records (remember intent=observation) land as
168	        # "observation". We select Minima outcomes by metadata kind instead.
169	        # prefer_current_run keeps everything in this lane's run while skipping
170	        # cross-run global-lesson overlays from other actors.
171	        call_kwargs = {
172	            "query": query,
173	            "session_id": lane,
174	            "lane": lane,
175	            "mode": self._settings.minima_recall_mode,
176	            "user_id": user_id,
177	            "limit": limit,
178	            "entry_types": list(entry_types) if entry_types else None,
179	            "include_working_memory": False,
180	            "prefer_current_run": True,
181	            "env_tags": list(env_tags) if env_tags else None,
182	        }
183	        try:
184	            with anyio.move_on_after(budget_ms / 1000.0) as scope:
185	                raw = await threadpool.run_cancellable(self._client.recall, **call_kwargs)
186	            if scope.cancelled_caught:
187	                log.warning("recall_timeout", lane=lane, budget_ms=budget_ms)
188	                return RecallResult(evidence=[], degraded=True, timed_out=True)
189	            return self._parse_recall(raw)
190	        except TransportError as exc:
191	            log.warning("recall_transport_error", lane=lane, code=exc.args[0] if exc.args else "")
192	            return RecallResult(evidence=[], degraded=True, error=str(exc))
193	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — recall must never break a recommendation
194	            log.warning("recall_error", lane=lane, error=str(exc))
195	            return RecallResult(evidence=[], degraded=True, error=str(exc))
196	
197	    def _parse_recall(self, raw: object) -> RecallResult:
198	        data: Mapping[str, Any] = raw if isinstance(raw, Mapping) else {}
199	        evidence: list[RecalledEvidence] = []
200	        for ev in data.get("evidence") or []:
201	            if not isinstance(ev, Mapping):
202	                continue
203	            evidence.append(
204	                RecalledEvidence(
205	                    entry_id=str(ev.get("id", "")),
206	                    reference_id=(ev.get("reference_id") or None),
207	                    score=_f(ev.get("score")),
208	                    knowledge_confidence=_f(ev.get("knowledge_confidence")),
209	                    is_stale=bool(ev.get("is_stale", False)),
210	                    content=str(ev.get("content", "")),
211	                    record=OutcomeRecord.from_metadata(ev.get("metadata_json")),
212	                )
213	            )
214	        return RecallResult(
215	            evidence=evidence,
216	            degraded=bool(data.get("degraded", False)),
217	            raw_confidence=_f(data.get("confidence")),
218	        )
219	
220	    async def get_context(
221	        self,
222	        *,
223	        query: str,
224	        lane: str,
225	        user_id: str | None = None,
226	        entry_types: Sequence[str] | None = None,
227	        max_token_budget: int = 1500,
228	    ) -> str:
229	        try:
230	            raw = await threadpool.run(
231	                self._client.get_context,
232	                query=query,
233	                session_id=lane,
234	                user_id=user_id,
235	                entry_types=list(entry_types or CONTEXT_ENTRY_TYPES),
236	                include_working_memory=False,
237	                max_token_budget=max_token_budget,
238	                format="structured",
239	                mode="full",
240	            )
241	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
242	            log.warning("get_context_error", lane=lane, error=str(exc))
243	            return ""
244	        if isinstance(raw, Mapping):
245	            return str(raw.get("context_block", ""))
246	        return ""
247	
248	    # ---- writes ----------------------------------------------------------------
249	
250	    async def remember_outcome(
251	        self,
252	        *,
253	        content: str,
254	        record: OutcomeRecord,
255	        lane: str,
256	        upsert_key: str,
257	        idempotency_key: str,
258	        user_id: str | None = None,
259	        env_tags: Sequence[str] | None = None,
260	        importance: str = "medium",
261	        source: str = "human",
262	    ) -> str | None:
263	        raw = await threadpool.run(
264	            self._client.remember,
265	            content=content,
266	            session_id=lane,
267	            agent_id="minima",
268	            intent="observation",
269	            metadata=record.to_metadata(),
270	            user_id=user_id,
271	            upsert_key=upsert_key,
272	            importance=importance,
273	            source=source,
274	            lane=lane,
275	            idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
276	            env_tags=list(env_tags) if env_tags else None,
277	            wait=True,
278	        )
279	        return _extract_record_id(raw)
280	
281	    async def record_outcome(
282	        self,
283	        *,
284	        lane: str,
285	        reference_id: str,
286	        outcome: str,
287	        signal: float,
288	        entry_ids: Sequence[str] | None = None,
289	        user_id: str | None = None,
290	        verified_in_production: bool = False,
291	        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
292	        rationale: str = "",
293	    ) -> dict:
294	        # Low-level control op so we can pass idempotency_key (the typed
295	        # client.record_outcome wrapper drops it).
296	        payload = {
297	            "run_id": lane,
298	            "reference_id": reference_id,
299	            "outcome": outcome,
300	            "signal": signal,
301	            "rationale": rationale,
302	            "user_id": user_id,
303	            "verified_in_production": verified_in_production or None,
304	            "entry_ids": list(entry_ids) if entry_ids else None,
305	            "idempotency_key": idempotency_key,
306	        }
307	        payload = {k: v for k, v in payload.items() if v is not None}
308	        raw = await threadpool.run(self._client._control.record_outcome, payload)
309	        return raw if isinstance(raw, dict) else {}
310	
311	    async def remember_lesson(
312	        self,
313	        *,
314	        content: str,
315	        lane: str,
316	        upsert_key: str,
317	        user_id: str | None = None,
318	        lesson_type: str = "success",
319	        lesson_scope: str = "session",
320	        importance: str = "high",
321	        env_tags: Sequence[str] | None = None,
322	        metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
323	        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
324	    ) -> str | None:
325	        # A Lesson entry (intent="lesson") goes through the server's validation gate and
326	        # feeds reflect()/surface_strategies rule promotion. upsert_key keeps one durable
327	        # lesson per (cluster, model) so repeated wins reinforce rather than flood LTM.
328	        raw = await threadpool.run(
329	            self._client.remember,
330	            content=content,
331	            session_id=lane,
332	            agent_id="minima",
333	            intent="lesson",
334	            lesson_type=lesson_type,
335	            lesson_scope=lesson_scope,
336	            lesson_importance=importance,
337	            metadata=dict(metadata) if metadata else None,
338	            user_id=user_id,
339	            upsert_key=upsert_key,
340	            importance=importance,
341	            source="human",
342	            lane=lane,
343	            idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
344	            env_tags=list(env_tags) if env_tags else None,
345	            wait=True,
346	        )
347	        return _extract_record_id(raw)
348	
349	    async def batch_insert(
350	        self, *, run_id: str, items: list[dict], deduplicate: bool = True
351	    ) -> dict:
352	        # Control batch_insert (/v2/control/batch_insert) is run-scoped and takes
353	        # {run_id, deduplicate, items}. The core route expects a bare array.
354	        raw = await threadpool.run(
355	            self._client._control.batch_insert,
356	            {"run_id": run_id, "deduplicate": deduplicate, "items": items},
357	        )
358	        return raw if isinstance(raw, dict) else {}
359	
360	    async def reflect(
361	        self, *, lane: str, user_id: str | None = None, include_linked_runs: bool = False
362	    ) -> dict:
363	        raw = await threadpool.run(
364	            self._client.reflect,
365	            session_id=lane,
366	            user_id=user_id,
367	            include_linked_runs=include_linked_runs,
368	        )
369	        return raw if isinstance(raw, dict) else {}
370	
371	    async def surface_strategies(
372	        self, *, lane: str, lesson_types: Sequence[str] | None = None, max_strategies: int = 5
373	    ) -> dict:
374	        raw = await threadpool.run(
375	            self._client.surface_strategies,
376	            session_id=lane,
377	            lesson_types=list(lesson_types) if lesson_types else None,
378	            max_strategies=max_strategies,
379	        )
380	        return raw if isinstance(raw, dict) else {}
381	
382	    @property
383	    def endpoint(self) -> str:
384	        return self._endpoint
385	
386	    async def health(self) -> dict:
387	        """Liveness probe via Mubit's core health route (no embedding, fast)."""
388	        base = self._endpoint.rstrip("/")
389	        url = f"{base}/v2/core/health"
390	        try:
391	            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=3.0) as http:
392	                resp = await http.get(url)
393	            return {
394	                "reachable": resp.status_code == 200,
395	                "transport": self._transport,
396	                "status_code": resp.status_code,
397	            }
398	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
399	            return {
400	                "reachable": False,
401	                "transport": self._transport,
402	                "error": str(exc),
403	            }
404	
405	
406	def _extract_record_id(raw: object) -> str | None:
407	    if not isinstance(raw, Mapping):
408	        return None
409	    traces = raw.get("traces") or []
410	    if not traces or not isinstance(traces[0], Mapping):
411	        return None
412	    writes = traces[0].get("writes") or []
413	    if not writes or not isinstance(writes[0], Mapping):
414	        return None
415	    record_id = writes[0].get("record_id")
416	    return str(record_id) if record_id else None
417
